title: Add message timestamps and reply button to conversation view

requirements.text: Display a timestamp on every message bubble in ConversationView showing when the message was sent or received. The timestamp should be formatted in a human-readable way (e.g., '2:45 PM' for today's messages, or 'Mon 2:45 PM' for older messages) and positioned below each message bubble.

quotes: show when messages sent and received

acceptanceCriteria: Every message bubble in ConversationView displays a timestamp below it
Timestamps show time only (e.g., '2:45 PM') for messages sent today
Timestamps show day and time (e.g., 'Mon 2:45 PM') for messages older than today
Timestamps are visually subtle (muted/small text) and do not overwhelm the message content
Both sent and received messages show timestamps

requirements.text: Add a Reply button to every message bubble in ConversationView. When clicked, it should populate the message input field with a quoted preview of the original message (e.g., prepend '> [original message text]\n' to the input) and focus the input so the user can type their reply.

quotes: add a reply button to every message

acceptanceCriteria: Every message bubble in ConversationView has a Reply button visible on hover or as a persistent icon
Clicking Reply populates the message input with a quoted reference to the original message content
The message input is focused automatically after clicking Reply
A visual indicator (e.g., a reply-to banner above the input) shows which message is being replied to, with an option to cancel the reply
The reply context is cleared after the reply message is sent

requirements.text: Ensure the message data model used in ConversationView exposes a timestamp field (createdAt or similar) from the backend so it can be rendered in the frontend. If the existing Message type already returns a timestamp, confirm it is passed through to the ConversationView component and each message bubble.

quotes: show when messages sent and received

acceptanceCriteria: The Message type in the backend includes a timestamp field (Int or Time)
The timestamp is returned in the conversation query results
The frontend ConversationView receives and renders the timestamp for each message

constraints: Do not modify files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui
Reply functionality should be client-side only (quoted context prepended to message text); do not add a separate reply thread data model to the backend unless the timestamp field is missing
nonGoals: Threaded reply chains or nested reply views
Read receipts or delivery status indicators beyond what already exists
